name: Chocolate Damnation
recipeInstructions:
Brownie Shell:
To make shell: Melt butter with chocolate in a saucepan over low heat. Remove from heat and add sugar and eggs. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt and beat into chocolate mixture. Add vanilla. Line a greased 15 x 10-inch jelly-roll pan with waxed paper. Pour in batter and spread evenly. Bake at 350°F for 12 to 15 minutes or until the cake springs back when lightly touched. The cake should be soft, not crisp. Turn out on a rack and cool.
Filling: Combine chocolate and coffee in the top of a double boiler and melt over hot water. Remove from heat. Beat egg yolks until pale yellow and stir in some of the chocolate mixture. Return to the chocolate in the pan and stirring until smooth. Gradually stir in liqueur and cool. Beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add sugar and beat until stiff. Whip cream until stiff. Fold cream into the cooled chocolate mixture, then fold in egg whites.
Invert the brownie shell onto a towel and peel off the paper. Line the bottom and sides of a greased 9-inch square baking dish with the brownie shell, cutting strips for sides and using the 9-inch square for the bottom. Turn filling into the cake-lined dish and cover with plastic wrap. Chill 3 to 4 hours or until firm.
Glaze: Combine chocolate and coffee in the top of a double boiler and melt over hot water.
Invert the chilled dessert onto a serving platter. Coat with chocolate glaze. Let glaze set and decorate with chocolate curls. Cut into very thin slices to serve.
